title: Glaucoma Optic-Disc Cupping Classification (PWDR)
sub_domain: Optic disc + cup segmentation from fundus with cup-to-disc-ratio glaucoma readout
source_data_quality_note: v2 PWDR Principle. Wraps L1-049 fundus (existing in v1 genesis) with deterministic cup-to-disc-ratio (CDR) threshold rules per ICO Glaucoma Guidelines 2016 to assign glaucoma suspect / glaucoma categorical labels. Universal in ophthalmology — every fundus exam reviewed for cupping; CDR is the primary morphologic biomarker for primary open-angle glaucoma (POAG) screening. UTSW Department of Ophthalmology glaucoma clinic high-volume use.

gate_substitutions.conservation_substitute: softmax simplex closure on glaucoma-suspect ordinal {normal, suspect, glaucoma_likely, advanced_glaucoma} per ICO 2016 + ISNT-rule

discrete_readout.threshold_function: Glaucoma-suspect(fundus) = step on (vertical_CDR, ISNT_rule_violation, neuroretinal_rim_thinning, RNFL_defect_presence, peripapillary_atrophy): normal if vertical_CDR < 0.5 + ISNT obeyed; suspect if 0.5 <= CDR < 0.7 OR mild ISNT violation; glaucoma_likely if CDR >= 0.7 OR significant rim notching; advanced if CDR >= 0.8 OR RNFL defect with corresponding visual field loss. ISNT rule: in normal eye, neuroretinal rim is thickest Inferiorly > Superiorly > Nasally > Temporally; violation suggests glaucomatous damage.
discrete_readout.underlying_physical_state: optic disc + optic cup segmentation masks + neuroretinal rim morphology + RNFL defect detection recovered from L1-049 fundus photograph (or sibling SLO / OCT-en-face)
discrete_readout.threshold_continuity_proof: Cup-to-disc ratio is continuous on disc + cup segmentation; ISNT-rule violations are step functions on rim-quadrant-thickness measurements. Smoothed-classification globally Lipschitz with constant ~0.05 per percentage-point of segmentation error.

related_principles: L1-049 (fundus — RGB fundus photography analytical core, physics_core_principle)
L1-042 (OCT — sibling RNFL thickness assessment, informational)
L1-513 (DR ETDRS PWDR — sibling fundus-based PWDR, d_principle ~ 0.45)

E.description: Glaucoma Optic-Disc Cupping (PWDR): wraps L1-049 with ICO 2016 Glaucoma Guidelines + Disc Damage Likelihood Scale grading rules. Stage 1 (analytical, from L1-049): segment optic disc and optic cup boundaries; measure vertical and horizontal CDR; assess neuroretinal rim morphology (ISNT pattern); detect peripapillary RNFL defects. Stage 2 (deterministic threshold): apply CDR + ISNT + DDLS rules. Difficulty tier delta = 3.
E.forward_model: Stage 1 (L1-049): fundus photograph -> {disc_mask, cup_mask, vertical_CDR, ISNT_rim_thickness, RNFL_defect_mask}. Stage 2: severity = step(features per ICO 2016 + ISNT + DDLS).
E.world_state_x: Per-eye glaucoma severity {normal, suspect, glaucoma_likely, advanced}.
E.observation_y: Disc-centered fundus photograph (typically 30-degree FOV centered on optic disc, 2000x2000 RGB).

G.dag: L.fundus_acquisition -> L.disc_cup_segmentation -> L.cdr_measurement -> L.isnt_rule_check -> L.rnfl_defect_detection -> L.glaucoma_severity_classifier -> int.spatial
coupling_constraints: disc_cup_to_features: disc + cup segmentation jointly constrains CDR and ISNT-rim-thickness measurements

W.regime: Existence inherited from L1-049. Uniqueness conditional on adequate disc visibility (no media opacity). Stability dominated by physiologic_cupping_variant (large physiologic cups can mimic glaucoma) and tilted_disc_anatomy. Joint Hadamard well-posedness established by Bourne 2016 (ICO Glaucoma Guidelines), Spaeth 2002 (Disc Damage Likelihood Scale), Jonas 1989 (foundational neuroretinal rim morphometry), Li 2018 (deep learning glaucoma detection benchmark).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + image-segmentation [disc + cup segmentation U-Net] + categorical-readout [ICO + ISNT classifier] | end-to-end deep neural [GlaucomaNet, EyeArt glaucoma]
C.error_bound: Inherits L1-049. Misclassification probability bound: Pr(severity_hat != severity_true) <= C * |CDR_error| / d(CDR, severity_threshold).
C.complexity: O(H * W) for segmentation; O(1) for classification

recast_attempted.recast_path: Original: 'Classify fundus into glaucoma vs normal'. Recast: 'Segment disc + cup via L1-049; compute CDR + check ISNT rule; apply ICO 2016 + DDLS thresholds'. Feasible because optic disc morphology has well-defined photometric forward model.

joint_well_posedness_references: Bourne RRA et al. (2016) ICO Guidelines for Glaucoma Eye Care. International Council of Ophthalmology.
Spaeth GL et al. (2002) The disc damage likelihood scale: reproducibility of a new method of estimating the amount of optic nerve damage caused by glaucoma. Trans Am Ophthalmol Soc 100:181-185.
Jonas JB, Gusek GC, Naumann GO (1989) Optic disc, cup and neuroretinal rim size, configuration and correlations in normal eyes. Invest Ophthalmol Vis Sci 29(7):1151-1158.
Li Z, He Y, Keel S, Meng W, Chang RT, He M (2018) Efficacy of a deep learning system for detecting glaucomatous optic neuropathy based on color fundus photographs. Ophthalmology 125(8):1199-1206.
Phene S et al. (2019) Deep learning and glaucoma specialists: the relative importance of optic disc features to predict glaucoma. Ophthalmology 126(12):1627-1639.

distinctness_audit.closest_existing_principle: L1-049 fundus (parent core) and L1-513 DR PWDR (sibling)
distinctness_audit.advisory_label: Distinct (parent-child PWDR; sibling DR PWDR has different lesion targets)
distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-049 fundus recovers full retinal photograph; L1-529 specializes to disc/cup segmentation + glaucoma classification. Distinct from L1-513 DR (different lesion set: vasculature/microaneurysms vs disc/cup/RNFL).

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Universal in ophthalmology. Glaucoma is the second leading cause of blindness globally. CDR + ISNT + RNFL is the morphologic foundation of POAG screening. UTSW Department of Ophthalmology glaucoma clinic. Deep learning systems (Li 2018, Phene 2019) achieve specialist-level diagnostic performance on benchmark datasets (REFUGE, ORIGA, RIM-ONE).
